title: Despite Chewy's Q3 Earnings Miss, Here's Why I'd Still Buy It
text: Chewy's third-quarter results disappointed investors. The e-commerce pet supply company reported earnings of $0.20 per share, falling short of the $0.23 consensus estimate, though revenue slightly exceeded expectations at nearly $2.9 billion. The company experienced a slight year-over-year decline in active customers (20.16 million), triggering a 7% stock decline. However, the market may be overlooking several positive indicators. Revenue topped expectations by a small margin with nearly 5% year-over-year top-line growth, continuing a multi-year streak of quarterly revenue increases. Earnings surged 33% year-over-year (from $0.15 to $0.20 per share), suggesting Chewy has reached operational scale where profitability is accelerating despite slower sales growth. Customer losses are slowing, with at 0.5% annual decline representing the slowest attrition rate in recent periods. On a sequential basis, customer counts are actually growing again. Meanwhile, per-customer spending continues rising. A significant strength is Chewy's growing autoship business—recurring subscriptions representing the vast majority of sales—which reduces marketing costs and builds customer loyalty. The piece emphasizes Chewy controls approximately one-third of the U.S. pet e-commerce market, positioned for growth as the overall market is projected to nearly double by 2030.
